_purpose: REFERENCE FILE -- not loaded by the engine. Copy parts you need into: asset-profiling-schema/endpoint.schema.custom.json. Loaded automatically by Get-SISchemaWithCustomMerge.ps1 and merged on top of asset-profiling-schema/endpoint.schema.locked.json.
_loadingNotes: Custom overlay:  asset-profiling-schema/endpoint.schema.custom.json   (you create -- this file is a sample of that)
fields[*]                          -- same name => REPLACES locked field, new name => APPENDS
aggregator.contributors[*]         -- same id   => REPLACES locked, new id => APPENDS
rawPayload.sources.<src>.includePaths[*]   -- UNION (locked first, then your additions)
rawPayload.sources.<new-src>       -- entirely new source bucket => ADDS
hashes / entityIds / aiEligibility -- engine invariants, CANNOT be overridden
_examplesIncluded: 1. Add a brand-new bool field with purpose=risk -- auto-included in RA queries
2. Override an existing field's tier mapping
3. Add a new aggregator contributor (your custom CMDB column)
4. Add includePaths to surface extra fields from raw payload

fields._comment: EXAMPLE 1: brand-new bool field. purpose=risk auto-includes it in every RA report's RiskFactor_Probability + RiskFactor_Probability_Detailed (no Build-RiskAnalysis.ps1 edit needed). Set fillFrom to the column in your raw payload that drives this bool.
fields.name: HasUnpatchedExploitedCVE
fields.description: True when the asset has at least one CVE flagged in CISA KEV catalog and patch is missing > 7 days.

fields._comment: EXAMPLE 2: brand-new int field surfaced from your CMDB join.
fields.name: cmdbServiceTierNumeric
fields.description: Numeric copy of cmdbServiceTier (1..5). Lets KQL filter without string parsing.

fields._comment: EXAMPLE 3: REPLACE an existing field's tier mapping. Same `name` as a locked field -> replaces in full. Use this carefully; you're taking ownership of every aspect of this field. Here we change a string default.
fields.name: OsPlatform
fields.description: Customer override of OsPlatform default (was empty string).

aggregator._comment: EXAMPLE 4: extend the aggregator to surface a custom CMDB column into a profile field. The contributor's id matches the field name above (cmdbServiceTierNumeric).

rawPayload._comment: EXAMPLE 5: surface extra fields from MDE's DeviceInfo into Properties.collect.mde -- they become available in KQL via column_ifexists on the Profile_CL table.

sources._comment_newSource: EXAMPLE 6: entirely new source bucket. Useful when you've added a custom enricher (e.g. ManageEngine, Tanium) -- the bucket appears under Properties.collect.<sourceName> in the Profile_CL table.
